title: Air-fryer halloumi
subtitle: Crisp halloumi with honey and lemon
excerpt: Halloumi slices cooked in the air fryer until golden and crisp on the outside and soft in the middle, finished with a drizzle of honey and a squeeze of lemon. Ready in 8 minutes. Serves four as a starter or side.
sourceNotes: Halloumi is a semi-hard Cypriot cheese made from a mixture of sheep and goat milk, with a high melting point that allows it to be fried or grilled. The air fryer replicates the effect of a dry griddle pan by circulating hot air at high speed, producing caramelisation without added fat. The honey and lemon finish is a contemporary British-Mediterranean serving convention.

content.text: Halloumi in the air fryer is faster than a frying pan and uses no extra oil. The cheese needs no coating and no preparation beyond slicing. The honey goes on at the end, not before: added earlier it burns. The lemon lifts the sweetness and cuts through the salt of the cheese.

Slice the halloumi into 1 cm rounds. Brush the air fryer basket with a little olive oil to prevent sticking. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C. Place the halloumi slices in a single layer. Cook for 7–8 minutes, turning halfway through, until golden on both sides with darker patches at the edges. Transfer to a plate immediately. Drizzle with {{honey}} of honey and a squeeze of lemon. Scatter with chilli flakes if using. Serve at once.

items.cause: Basket not oiled, or halloumi placed on a cold basket.
items.fix: Brush the basket with oil before preheating, or use perforated air fryer paper. The halloumi releases easily once a crust forms — do not force it.

items.symptom: Cheese pale and rubbery rather than golden
items.cause: Temperature too low or time too short.
items.fix: Ensure the air fryer is fully preheated to 200°C. Halloumi needs direct, high heat to colour — at 180°C it heats through without crisping.

items.symptom: Cheese dry and tough rather than soft inside
items.fix: Check at 6 minutes. The window between golden and overdone is narrow. Serve immediately after cooking — halloumi hardens as it cools.

content.text: Air-fryer halloumi works as a starter with flatbread and salad, as a protein in grain bowls, or alongside roasted vegetables. The honey-lemon combination is the default, but the cooked cheese also pairs well with pomegranate molasses, za'atar, or a spoonful of hot sauce. It is best eaten within 5 minutes of coming out of the air fryer.
